mechanism_narrative: High and rising federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise and future cash flows are impaired.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic growth concerns mount.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based weakness in consumer demand and investment, signaling an impending economic slowdown that negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show significant declines as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, forcing the central bank to maintain tight monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns through higher discount rates and reduced earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3% year-over-year, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust economic environment, supporting corporate earnings and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns as future growth prospects improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer base, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns due to increased demand and economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining output, indicating a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced supply-side activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is high and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low, it indicates a tight monetary policy in a strong labor market, often preceding an economic slowdown as policy bites, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% and UNRATE is below 4.5%.
